title: Figure 3 in The potential utility of postnatal skeletal developmental patterns in squamate phylogenetics
author: Maisano, Jessica A.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 3. Results of sequence unit analysis with polymorphisms coded by the frequency method of Prober et al. (1990) and Wiens (1993). A, strict consensus of two equally parsimonious trees that result when Bipes biporus is included. B, single most parsimonious tree that results when B. biporus is excluded. Tree length = 2901 steps, CI = 0.40, RI = 0.51. Node 1, Gekkota; node 2, Scincidae; node 3, Xantusiidae.
steps_raw: Figure 3. Results of sequence unit analysis with polymorphisms coded by the frequency method of Prober et al. (1990) and Wiens (1993). A, strict consensus of two equally parsimonious trees that result when Bipes biporus is included. B, single most parsimonious tree that results when B. biporus is excluded. Tree length = 2901 steps, CI = 0.40, RI = 0.51. Node 1, Gekkota; node 2, Scincidae; node 3, Xantusiidae.
